workSummary: Initiated work on internship learning modules by exploring the fundamental concepts related to the assigned domain. Reviewed introductory materials, documentation, and structured content to understand the scope of the internship and the type of problems being addressed.
learningOutcomes: Gained clarity on internship expectations and developed an initial understanding of domain-specific concepts and workflows.

workSummary: Continued studying core concepts and began analyzing how theoretical knowledge can be translated into practical implementations. Focused on understanding system-level thinking and how different components interact within a workflow.

workSummary: Worked on applying initial concepts through small practical exercises and guided examples. Focused on understanding input-output relationships and how logic flows within a system.
learningOutcomes: Improved ability to map theoretical concepts into simple working solutions.

workSummary: Explored problem-solving techniques using logical constructs and structured approaches. Practiced breaking complex problems into manageable components.

workSummary: Focused on understanding system design at a basic level, including how data flows through different stages and how outputs are generated based on conditions.

workSummary: Worked on programming fundamentals, including writing basic structured code and understanding syntax across multiple programming environments.

workSummary: Implemented loops and iterative processes to handle repetitive tasks and improve efficiency in code execution.

workSummary: Worked on functions and modular programming techniques to organize code into reusable components.

workSummary: Solved structured problems using previously learned concepts and practiced implementing logic across different scenarios.

workSummary: Reviewed previously learned concepts and reinforced knowledge through practice exercises and revisions.
